title: Intent Drift Review
description: Schema for a structured intent drift review result, shared by internal (LLM-generated) and external (human-submitted) reviews.

intent_drift_review_id.description: Unique identifier for the drift review record.

plan_attempt_id.description: The plan attempt this review is associated with.

intent_thread_id.description: The intent thread this review is part of.

root_intent_packet_id.description: The root intent packet ID for lineage verification.

reviewed_intent_packet_id.description: The specific intent packet that was reviewed.

review_packet_hash.description: SHA256 hash of the review packet at the time of review.

review_source.description: Whether the review was submitted externally (human) or generated internally (LLM).

submitted_by.description: The identity that submitted or generated this review. Internal reviews use relay-internal-drift-reviewer.

source_artifact_path.description: Optional path to the source artifact for this review.

overall_alignment.description: The reviewer's overall assessment of intent-to-plan alignment.

confidence.description: Reviewer confidence in the assessment, in [0, 1].

findings.description: Structured findings from the review. May be empty only when overall_alignment=aligned and recommended_action=approve.

location.description: Optional location reference within the plan.

recommended_action.description: The action recommended by the reviewer.

approval_gate_status.description: The approval gate status derived from the recommended action.

model_metadata.description: Model metadata. Required for internal reviews; may be null for external reviews.

provider.description: Model provider identifier (e.g. openai, anthropic, fake).

model_tier.description: The model tier used for this review call.

temperature.description: The temperature used for this review call.

input_hash.description: SHA256 hash of the input payload sent to the model.

output_hash.description: SHA256 hash of the raw output received from the model.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes or reasoning from the reviewer.
